summary: The test suite has strong coverage of the main PR label assignment requirements (glob patterns, label merging, priority ordering, JSON output) across a well-structured three-tier approach: unit tests, file-driven integration tests, and end-to-end testing via GitHub Actions (act). Coverage of core functionality is comprehensive (4/5) and test organization is clear with good separation of concerns (4/5). However, rigor is limited (3/5). Notably missing: the `?` glob character is unsupported in tests despite being in the implementation, JSON error scenarios (invalid JSON, malformed rules, missing fields) are not tested, edge cases like empty file arrays are absent, special characters in file paths are untested, and rule field validation is not covered. The act harness efficiently uses 3 minimal cases (docs-only, mixed, no-match) but lacks scenario diversity. Error handling testing is minimal (only missing files). These gaps mean the suite would reliably catch regressions in happy-path label assignment but might miss edge cases and error conditions in production.
